channel.title: NASA 新闻 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: NASA 官方新闻 + 英译简

item.title: 我是阿尔忒弥斯：杰森·彼得森
item.description:
贾森·彼得森 (Jason Peterson) 对 NASA 阿尔忒弥斯 II 任务的职责超出了他在密西西比州圣路易斯湾附近的 NASA 斯坦尼斯航天中心担任靶场运营经理的通常职责。
对于彼得森来说，在 NASA 工作是一个超现实的完整循环时刻。
“我在密西西比州的德莱尔长大，就在美国宇航局的附近，”彼得森说。 “你会听到美国宇航局的测试，窗户会摇晃，你知道它们就在那里。一百万年后我从来没有梦想过我会与美国宇航局合作。”
在进入航天局之前，他的职业生涯经历了几个转折。
“我从学校毕业，做了焊接工作，上了大学，然后加入了空军。通往 NASA 的道路真正从那里开始发展，”彼得森说。
彼得森在美国空军和空军国民警卫队服役了 24 年，从事机场和空对地作战工作，随后在空客总装线担任飞机调度员几年。
彼得森将他的军事经验归功于他为 12 至 16 小时的工作日做好了准备，这帮助阿耳忒弥斯二号绕月试飞并进入了世界各地观众的视野。
他支持负责为 NASA 的 SLS（太空发射系统）火箭与猎户座飞船上的阿耳忒弥斯二号机组人员一起发射视频和通信提供支持的团队，然后降落在太平洋。
在近 10 天的任务中，NASA 宇航员 Reid Wiseman、Victor Glover、Christina Koch 和 CSA（加拿大航天局）宇航员 Jeremy Hansen 完成了创纪录的飞越月球，距离地球最远距离为 252,756 英里。
在约翰·P·穆萨号航空母舰上，彼得森是第一个在船上发现太空舱与船员一起返回的人。
NASA Stennis 靶场运营经理
彼得森操作六台摄像机中的一台，通过使用具有热功能的高分辨率、多传感器系统提供实时报道。利用飞船的航向和猎户座的预测轨迹，他在几分钟内找到了太空舱并开始跟踪它。
猎户座在距地球表面约 400,000 英尺处进入地球大气层，以近 35 倍音速行进，距加利福尼亚海岸附近的溅落点约 1,956 英里。彼得森跟踪猎户座从释放小降落伞（首先使太空舱减速并稳定），到主降落伞打开，一直到溅落。
“你完全锁定，保持中心、焦点和稳定，”彼得森说。
几周前，彼得森在位于佛罗里达州的美国宇航局肯尼迪航天中心开始关注这次任务。
在发射前几天，彼得森与团队合作进行全面的系统检查、部署通信设备以及准备无人机和固定翼摄像机资产。他帮助配置和操作系统，确保发射台、人群以及周围空中和地面平台的实时视频传输。
4 月 1 日，Artemis II 发射日，团队卸下并安装了高分辨率传感器、连接终端、路由器、监视器、光纤线路和无人机设备。他们在升空前驾驶无人机捕捉人群场景和鸟瞰图。
当 SLS 火箭发射时，彼得森的全部注意力都集中在无人机的定位和保持射击上。
“没有太多时间退后一步，把握当下，因为你要专注于精确性，”彼得森说。
随着任务的进行，彼得森和团队收拾好行李，返回设施并下载了当天的镜头。
当彼得森于 4 月 4 日抵达圣地亚哥时，阿耳忒弥斯二号船员已经到达月球一半以上。在溅落前的几天，该团队装载并安装了高保真摄像机和支持设备，所有这些都是为了帮助全世界见证阿耳忒弥斯二号船员于 4 月 10 日返回地球时的历史性里程碑。
接下来是阿耳忒弥斯三号。 NASA 将使用 SLS 火箭将四名来自 NASA 肯尼迪的宇航员送上猎户座飞船。以四月份成功的阿耳忒弥斯二号载人试飞为基础，阿耳忒弥斯三号将为未来的地面任务铺平道路，彼得森等专家将在每一步提供帮助。
阿耳忒弥斯二号的科学调查结果将有助于支持人类安全探索深空……

item.title: 哈勃详细介绍了早期星系改变邻里的情况
item.description:
Astronomers using NASA’sHubble Space Telescopehave found something they never expected — ultraviolet light from a galaxy that existed just 1.4 billion years after the big bang. That galaxy contains tightly clustered young stars that produce ionizing light capable of transforming the opaque, neutral gas within and immediately around the galaxy, clearing our view. This suggests that similar galaxies in the early universe were responsible for clearing the neutral fog of hydrogen gas that once filled the cosmos.
Apaperdescribing this discovery was published June 23 in the Astrophysical Journal.
The galaxy, cataloged MXDFz4.4, existed at the end of theEra of Reionization, a transformative period in our universe. During roughly the first billion years of the cosmos, the gas between stars and galaxies was opaque to energetic ultraviolet light. As time wore on, gas everywhere became transparent or ionized. The changeover was not like an on/off switch, but likely took hundreds of millions of years. Researchers are still collecting evidence to fully understand how this happened, which is why MXDFz4.4 sets a critical precedent.
“Observing a galaxy like this was thought to be impossible,” said lead author Ilias Goovaerts, a postdoctoral fellow at the Space Telescope Science Institute (STScI) in Baltimore. “Researchers expected the ‘fog’ or neutral hydrogen that filled the early universe would be too thick and obscure our view of its ionizing light. Hubble not only spotted that light, but it also helped reveal incredible details about the galaxy’s characteristics.”
Young, massive stars emit ultraviolet light capable of ionizing hydrogen atoms. As this light traveled for over 12 billion years to reach Hubble, space expanded, and the light stretched orredshiftedinto visible light. Hubble’s wavelength coverage, combined with the sensitivity and resolution of its space-based vantage point, makes it the only telescope capable of capturing this ultraviolet light from the early universe.
“Astronomers have found many galaxies that existed at this point in the history of the universe, but we haven’t detectedionizingphotonsfrom any of them, making MXDFz4.4 one of a kind,” said Marc Rafelski, a co-author and Hubble deputy mission head at STScI.
Hubble’s long exposures, pulled from several existing surveys, revealed that the galaxy’s young, massive stars are the source of the ultraviolet light, which cleared the surrounding space. These stars formed in bursts within the last few million years of MXDFz4.4’s existence and are crammed together.
Amplifying this crowding effect, MXDFz4.4 is about 100 times smaller by area than our Milky Way galaxy, but is forming stars 10 times faster.
“A lot of young, hot, massive stars in a small space do a better job of blasting through opaque gas,” Goovaerts said. The researchers estimate that 50 to 100% of the young stars’ energetic ionizing light is escaping the surrounding gas.
Massive stars’ lifetimes also play a role, since they live for only a few million years. Many explode as supernovae, releasing gigantic amounts of energy and blowing colossal holes that allow even more light to escape.
Hubble could not do this alone. These conclusions are supported by survey data taken by NASA’sJames Webb Space Telescopein near-infrared light and the MUSE eXtremely Deep Field or MXDF, the galaxy’s namesake, captured by the European Southern Observatory’s Very Large Telescope (VLT) in visible light.
The team used Webb’s data to determine the galaxy’s mass, analyze its older stars, and measure the galaxy’s star formation history. The galaxy’s older stars are less massive and cooler, and therefore not responsible for changing the gas around them.
Comparing Hubble and Webb data also showed that recent star formation happened in bursts. “Without Webb to clarify what we saw in Hubble’s images, we couldn’t make these conclusions,” Rafelski said.
Data from the VLT pinpointed when MXDFz4.4 existed: 1.4 billion years after the big bang. Before this discovery, researchers had only identified a galaxy emitting ionized light from a time when the universe was 1.6 billion years old. Only a few additional examples have been identified, and those existed when the universe was about 2 billion years old. MXDFz4.4 brings researchers closer to drawing firm conclusions about how the Era of Reionization unfolded.
Studying the Era of Reionization is a decades-old endeavor. Researchers use statistics about star populations in nearby galaxies, which we canobserve in great detail, to make well-informed assumptions about what might be happening in galaxies in the early universe, in part because theirstar populations are too distant to resolve in any detail.
In 2023,researchers using Webbshowed that galaxies’ stars emitted enough light to heat and ionize the gas around them 900 million years after the big bang. This was a breakthrough, but astronomers need galaxies like MXDFz4.4 to fully explain how the process happened

item.title: 美国宇航局韦伯精确定位雪茄星系内数百万颗恒星
item.description:
Located 12 million light-years away and undergoing rapid star formation, edge-on spiral galaxy Messier 82 (M82) is a scientifically unique sight to behold, and now NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope has revealed previously unseen details.
M82’s intense star formation, thought to be the result of a galaxy merger, will be a short-lived event in astronomical terms, estimated to last a few hundred million years in its entirety. This temporary phase of extreme star formation relative to the galaxy’s mass, as well as its location in the local universe, are among the factors that make M82, also known as the Cigar galaxy, a one-of-a-kind environment to study.
A team of astronomers recently completed an imaging survey with the Webb telescope.This programentailed a total of 65 hours of observation time with Webb’sNIRCam (Near-Infrared Camera)instrument and revealed never-seen-before details of thestarburst galaxy, including its distended disk structure and millions of individual stars. Webb’s high-resolution imaging, specifically of the main plane of the galactic disk, has unlocked vital information for astronomers as they seek to uncover M82’s formation history. Additionally, the Webb data will help scientists understand the current processes occurring within the starburst galaxy.
“M82 is a mess, but it’s a beautiful mess. We don’t fully understand what’s going on, especially concerning its evolutionary history. What could have triggered such an elevated rate of star formation? How long has this galaxy been driving plumes of material away from its center?” said principal investigator Adam Smercina, a NASA Hubble Fellow at the Space Telescope Science Institute in Baltimore, and incoming Assistant Professor at Tufts University in Massachusetts. “M82 is an ideal galaxy evolution laboratory because it has properties that allow us to probe important physical processes, such as how stars form in such environments and how that activity drives outflows. M82 provides a simultaneous window onto many astrophysical questions, in a way that no other galaxy in the local universe can.”
Prior to Webb, many observatories looked at the starburst galaxy, includingNASA’s Hubbleand retired Spitzer space telescopes. However, the sheer volume of dust within that galaxy limited the amount of information astronomers could acquire on M82 at high resolution. While Webb has previouslylooked at this galaxy, the duration of the new imaging survey, combined with the telescope’s infrared sensitivity, enabled it to pierce through the thick dust.
The telescope’s near-infrared-light view is a snapshot of a scene that has been evolving over a couple hundred million years. Webb’s image contains approximately 16.5 million individual stars dispersed throughout the galaxy. The light from these stellar sources is depicted as luminous blue granules. This is only a small portion of the total amount of stars astronomers think reside in a galaxy like M82, with the majority too faint to be seen.
“The sheer number of stars that we were able to resolve with Webb is incredible,” said team member Benjamin Williams of the University of Washington. “It’s a whole different world from what we’ve been able to see with other telescopes. All of these stars collectively provide a detailed fossil record of the formation and evolution of M82.”
Moving inward, the increase in brightness and the asymmetrical shape of the galactic disk hints at the spiral galaxy’s unique underlying structure. The differing radii between the two sides suggests that M82 has a distorted shape, which can happen during intense galaxy mergers.
“At first glance, the disk of the galaxy may seem less spectacular because Webb sees through the dust,” said team member Eric Bell of the University of Michigan. “But M82 is a delightfully complex system. Webb’s observations will help us address some ongoing mysteries, such as how star formation has moved within M82 over the last few billion years.”
To view this video please enable JavaScript, and consider upgrading to a web browser thatsupports HTML5 video
Because of the extreme star formation within the galaxy, which is 10 times faster than the Milky Way galaxy’s star formation rate, stellar birth will eventually be disrupted. M82’s stellar frenzy is causing bipolar plumes of material to be ejected above and below the disk. Though it looks like a tumultuous region, the hourglass-shaped outflows appear to have a layered structure. The yellow tendrils of material closest to the galaxy’s disk represent ionized gas, whereas the orange material farther away depicts small dust grains. These grains are called polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons and are helpful in tracing material in the space between the galaxy’s stars, also known as the interstellar medium.
The information collected as part of this Webb study is just one dataset scientists will analyze as they seek to piece together this starburst galaxy’s formation history.
“Galaxies are such intricate ecos

item.title: 奈瓦沙湖上升水域沼泽
item.description:
Relentless rains are threatening a lake in Kenya's Great Rift Valley that has become a key hub in the global flower trade.
Kenya'sLake Naivashahas long been a place of change and reinvention.
In precolonial times, the nomadicMaasai peopleused the lake and surrounding grasslands to water and raise cattle during the dry season. The Maasai were eventually displaced by British colonists in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, including a group of free-thinking aristocrats who arrived in large numbers in the 1920s through 1940s. Known as theHappy Valley set,these newcomers cultivated lavish estates and were notorious for reveling in a culture of excess. Their influence faded in the 1950s and 1960samid scandaland the overthrow of colonial rule, allowing the area to transform into a center of wildlife tourism,flower farming, and geothermal energy production.
Now the lake faces another major change: rapidly fluctuating water levels. The name Naivasha comes from a Maasai word meaning "that which heaves," an apt description of the freshwater lake over the past 25 years. Satellite altimetry measurements of the lake's depth indicate an increase of about7 meters (23 feet)since 2010, roughly the height of a two-story building. Over the same period, Landsat observed a roughly 40 percent increase in the lake's area, adding 50 square kilometers (19 square miles) of water, equivalent to roughly 15 Central Parks.
The human and economic toll of the rising water levels is considerable, said Mathew Herrnegger, a hydrologist at BOKU University in Vienna, Austria. Homes, flower farms, and roads along the shores have all flooded in recent years, displacinglarge numbersof people. Lake Oloidien, once a separate lake, has effectively merged with Naivasha, bringing an influx of saline, alkaline water to Naivasha's freshwater system.
The Landsat images above compare the same area in January 2010 (left) and January 2026 (right), illustrating the scope of the changes. Neighborhoods in the southwestern part of the town of Naivasha have been particularly hard hit. Flooding has been widespread in the neighborhood ofKihoto, with entire town blocks inundated, including police stations, churches, hotels, restaurants, electrical power substations, and sewer systems.
"Increased rainfall is the primary driver," Herrnegger said. Mean annual rainfall rose by about 30 percent between 2010 and 2020 compared to the preceding decade, with a 318 percent increase in high-intensity rainfall, he said. Because the lake lies in a closed basin and has no surface outflow, it is especially sensitive to even modest changes in the water balance. Herrnegger and colleagues estimate that a 0.4–2.0 percent increase in annual rainfall is sufficient to explain the dramatic rises. "It is a system that, once tipped, responds strongly," he said.
The flower industry around the lake, which produces hundreds of millions of dollars in exports per year, is losing greenhouses, farmland, packing sheds, and worker housing on a significant scale. In communities such as Sulmac Village, Karagita, and Kasarani, many greenhouses that just a decade ago were set back a kilometer or more from the water now have lakeshore views.
Crescent Island—once a peninsula along the lake’s southern shore near several former Happy Valley estates and country clubs—is now primarily a game sanctuary and wildlife tourism destination. Hundreds of hippos live in the lake, and people, especially commercial fishermen, areencountering themwith more frequency as waters rise.
The images also show the expansion of aquatic vegetation, especiallywater hyacinth. Remote sensingscientistsandjournalistshave documented a rapid proliferation of the plant over the past two decades, which hasinterfered with fishingand tourism and possibly contributed to the lake's rising water levels by slowing evaporation.
Other researchers have pointed totectonic influenceschanging the rate of groundwater recharge into the lake as a possible contributing factor. In addition, the accumulation ofsedimentmay be filling shallow areas and reshaping the lake floor such that water levels may be rising and increasing the lake extent even though the volume of water held by the lake may be unchanged, explained Jamie Shutler, a professor of earth and environmental science at the University of Exeter in England.
"Given the large number of people who rely on this lake for food and their livelihoods, the change we're seeing from the satellite imagery combined with the stories of displacement is alarming," Shutler said. "We need more research to assess exactly how much the volume of water is changing each year and why."
NASA Earth Observatory images by Lauren Dauphin, using Landsat data from theU.S. Geological Surveyand water level data fromGlobal Water Measurements. Story by Adam Voiland.
Stay up-to-date with the latest content from NASA as we explore the universe and discover more about our home planet.
Lake Unter-See in Antarctica, s

item.title: NASA 授予联邦企业采购合同解决方案
item.description:
NASA 将开始处理企业范围采购解决方案 (SEWP) VI 政府范围采购合同的多个合同的授予。该合同提供了对商业产品和服务的简化访问，包括硬件、软件、云服务、网络安全工具、工程和咨询服务以及数据密集型任务支持能力。
此次竞争性收购分为三个类别：A 类，IT 解决方案； B类，企业级IT服务解决方案； C 类，基于 IT 任务的服务。
如需了解 SEWP VI 获奖者的完整名单以及其他计划详情，请访问：
所有合同均为无限期交付/无限数量合同，能够发出固定价格、工时、时间和材料以及其他定价安排任务订单。有效订购期限为10年，自11月1日起至2036年10月31日，每份合同的最高价值为200亿美元。
有关 NASA 和机构项目的信息，请访问：
Jennifer Dooren / Jessica Taveau华盛顿总部202-358-1600jennifer.m.dooren@nasa.gov/jessica.c.taveau@nasa.gov

item.title: 美国宇航局探空火箭将启动学生实验
item.description:
美国宇航局位于弗吉尼亚州的瓦洛普斯飞行设施计划于 6 月 24 日星期三美国东部时间上午 5:30 至 9:30 之间发射一枚探空火箭，运载学生为该机构的 RockSatX 和 RockOn 项目开发的实验，并于 6 月 25 日星期四进行备份。
RockSat 和 RockOn 计划提供技术培训和实践经验，为学生进入美国航空航天业做好准备和装备。 NASA 首次将 RockSat 和 RockOn 任务合并到一枚火箭中，该火箭将承载来自 38 个大学和社区学院团队的近 250 名参与者开发的实验。
NASA Wallops 劳动力发展负责人维多利亚·斯托菲尔 (Victoria Stoffel) 表示：“我们面临的挑战是找到方法，将尽可能多的实验安装到一枚探空火箭上。” “探空火箭项目办公室团队找到了创造性的方法，将近 50 个实验放入一枚火箭中。我们感谢 Wallops 团队实现这一目标，让学生们从这次体验中获得最大收益。”
RockOn 团队共同努力在现场构建实验，获得从头开始组装电路板并将其发射到太空的实践经验。更先进的 RockSat 项目团队设计和构建他们的实验，并根据 NASA 更大的任务进行设计审查。每个团队都可以体验真正的 NASA 任务，从开发到发射的整个过程。
RockSat 学生的实验范围从测量地球高层大气的天气和辐射到测试技术，例如隔热罩、空间碎片跟踪和机器人服务，这些都可以帮助 NASA 未来的任务。
将携带实验的梗改进型马勒穆特亚轨道探空火箭预计将达到约 100 英里的高度，然后通过降落伞降落到大西洋进行回收。此次发射在切萨皮克湾地区可能可见。
瓦洛普斯游客中心的启动观看区将于 6 月 24 日凌晨 4:30 开放供观看。直播将在发布前约 10 分钟在 Wallops YouTube 频道上开始。还可通过该设施的 Facebook 页面获取启动更新信息。
有关 NASA 探空火箭计划的更多信息，请访问：
作者：Jamie Adkins 美国宇航局弗吉尼亚州瓦勒普斯飞行设施

item.title: 美国宇航局邀请媒体参加博茨瓦纳阿耳忒弥斯协议签署仪式
item.description:
博茨瓦纳共和国将于美国东部时间 6 月 25 日星期四上午 9:30 在华盛顿 NASA 总部举行的仪式上签署《阿耳忒弥斯协议》。
美国宇航局副局长马特·安德森 (Matt Anderson) 将接待博茨瓦纳通信与创新部长戴维·采尔 (David Tshere) 和美国国务院太空高级顾问格雷戈里·奥特里 (Gregory Autry) 出席此次活动。
此活动仅限本人参加。有兴趣参加的媒体必须在下午 5 点之前回复。 6 月 24 日星期三，发送至：hq-media@mail.nasa.gov。 NASA 的媒体认证政策已上线。
2020年，在特朗普第一届政府执政期间，美国在美国宇航局和国务院的领导下，与其他七个创始国共同制定了《阿耳忒弥斯协议》，以回应政府和私营公司对月球活动日益增长的兴趣。
这些协议引入了第一套实用原则，旨在提高月球、火星及其他地区民用太空探索的安全性、透明度和协调性。博茨瓦纳将成为第68个签署《阿耳忒弥斯协议》的国家。
卡米尔·加洛 / 詹妮弗·多伦总部，华盛顿202-358-1600camille.m.gallo@nasa.gov/jennifer.m.dooren@nasa.gov

item.title: NASA 实验制造部门推动飞机创新
item.description:
在 NASA，创新早在飞机起飞之前就开始了。位于加利福尼亚州爱德华兹的美国宇航局阿姆斯特朗飞行研究中心的实验制造部门将工程概念转化为用于研究飞机和技术开发的任务就绪硬件。这种能力有助于该机构通过提高航空安全、效率和可持续性来实现造福公众的进步。
该分公司是 NASA 阿姆斯特朗研究和科学飞机机队的全方位服务制造、改装和维修中心。该团队专注于精密加工、钣金成型、飞机管材、焊接、增材制造、复合材料制造以及结构维修和改造。他们广泛的专业知识使他们能够为航空航天和地面应用构建定制硬件。
该分公司的工程技术人员拥有数十年的制造大师经验。他们设计和制造独特的组件、快速原型和飞行关键结构，满足 NASA 严格的安全和性能标准。无论是手工塑造复合结构还是生产精密铣削零件，团队在制造每个组件时都会牢记使命的成功。
实验制造支持 NASA 的广泛研究工作。当 NASA 阿姆斯特朗团队设计 AIRVUE（城市环境真实视频机载仪器）传感器吊舱以支持自主飞行研究时，制造团队将数字设计转换为功能齐全的结构。他们确保吊舱在部署进行试飞之前满足严格的安全要求。
除了任务支持之外，实验制造部门还为 NASA 的 STEM 参与工作做出了贡献。在当地的机器人竞赛中，技术人员使用移动制造设备来修复学生制造的机器人并展示加工和焊接技术。这些演示向学生介绍 NASA 的技术职业道路，并展示先进制造如何支持航空航天研究。
该分支机构使用现代计算机辅助设计和计算机辅助制造工具，包括 Pro E/Creo、MasterCam 和 SolidWorks，将数字模型转换为硬件。这种工程和制造的早期集成有助于缩短开发时间并减少设计与硬件的不匹配。与多个承包商之间工作转换的环境不同，NASA 阿姆斯特朗包括从早期设计到最终组装和飞机安装的制造团队。这种持续参与加强了与工程团队和飞行运营的协调。
最近的项目，例如先进的机翼模型制造和定制的轻型飞机地板，凸显了该部门在 NASA 阿姆斯特朗任务中的重要作用。无论是支持实验飞机、实现新技术演示，还是指导学生动手制造，实验制造部门都有助于推进 NASA 的使命，造福所有人。

item.title: 美国宇航局钱德拉发现可能的超新星遗迹
item.description:
利用美国宇航局钱德拉 X 射线天文台的数据，天文学家可能在银河系中部一个有趣的区域发现了一颗超新星遗迹（如这张 2026 年 6 月 11 日的图像中所示）。超新星遗迹是恒星爆炸后不断膨胀的残骸，提供铁、氧和硅等元素，这些元素对于行星的形成以及我们所知的生命的形成和繁荣至关重要。
如果得到证实，这个新的超新星遗迹将是迄今为止发现的最接近银河系中心区域超大质量黑洞的遗迹之一，这是一个充满了巨大恒星、长长的磁场线和围绕银河系中心快速轨道运行的密集气体云的奇异区域。
图片来源：-ray：NASA/CXC/UCLA/Z。朱等人； ESA/XMM-牛顿；光学：PanSTARRS；电台：MeerKAT；红外（JWST）：NASA/ESA/CSA/STScI；图像处理：NASA/CXC/SAO/L。弗拉塔尔和 P.埃德蒙兹

item.title: NASA 将报道美国太空行走 95，并举办预览新闻发布​​会
item.description:
NASA 宇航员将于 6 月 30 日星期二在国际空间站外冒险，更换轨道综合体 Canadaarm2 机械臂上的腕关节。太空行走计划于美国东部时间上午 8:35 左右开始。
来自 NASA 和 CSA（加拿大航天局）的专家将在下午 2 点的新闻发布会上预览即将到来的太空行走。 6 月 25 日星期四，在该机构的 YouTube 频道上。简报会在美国宇航局位于休斯敦的约翰逊航天中心举行。了解如何通过各种平台（包括社交媒体）观看 NASA 内容。
有兴趣亲自参加的美国媒体必须在下午 5 点之前联系约翰逊新闻编辑室。 6 月 24 日星期三，地址：jsccommu@mail.nasa.gov。通过电话加入的媒体应在同一截止日期前索取拨入详细信息。如需提问，媒体必须在新闻发布会开始前 15 分钟内拨打电话。
NASA 宇航员克里斯·威廉姆斯 (Chris Williams) 和杰西卡·梅尔 (Jessica Meir) 将离开空间站的 Quest 气闸，更换腕关节，该腕关节在 5 月 27 日加拿大臂 2 号正常运行期间出现故障，原因是该臂吸收了较高的电机电流并且未按预期移动。
从美国东部时间上午 7 点开始，在 NASA+、Amazon Prime、Netflix 和该机构的 YouTube 频道上观看 NASA 的美国太空行走 95 直播报道。太空行走预计将持续大约六个半小时。
NASA 与 CSA 合作了解了这个问题，并确定需要进行太空行走，以使用空间站上已有的备件更换接头。像 Canadaarm2 这样的机器人在连续运行超过 25 年之后，进行维修是正常现象，也是预料之中的事情，因为该系统在设计时就考虑到了可更换组件和计划维护。
这次太空行走将是威廉姆斯的第二次，也是梅尔的第五次。威廉姆斯将担任太空行走一号机组成员，并身穿红色条纹套装。梅厄将担任二号机组人员，并穿着无标记的套装。这将是第 280 次太空行走，旨在支持空间站组装、维护和升级。
要了解有关国际空间站研究、运营及其机组人员的更多信息，请访问：
吉米·拉塞尔华盛顿总部202-358-1100james.j.russell@nasa.gov
桑德拉·琼斯休斯顿约翰逊航天中心281-483-5111sandra.p.jones@nasa.gov
